title: OCR Error Detection Hotfix
body:
Summary of changes:
- Fix input handling of the error-detection model used for OCR text processing.
- Segment length is now managed by the tokenizer via token counts (#tokens) rather than character counts (#chars).
- Remove internal splitting logic that segmented inputs by character length within the marker pipeline.
- Join lines with a newline separator ("\n") when constructing inputs for the LLM, ensuring per-line processing remains aligned with the model expectations.

Rationale:
- Token-based segmentation aligns with common LLM tokenization practices and avoids brittle, character-level split logic that can disrupt line integrity across languages and encodings.
- Joining lines with explicit newlines ensures the model receives well-delimited per-line context, which improves per-line rewriting fidelity and downstream span construction.

Impact:
- Changes are isolated to the OCR/text processing and LLM augmentation paths, with no changes to the underlying rendering or storage formats.
- Tests and integration points that rely on the previous line-splitting behavior may need minor adjustments to inputs to the LLM prompts.

step_plan.summary: OCR Error Detection Hotfix: switch input assembly to token-based per-line processing, remove character-based splitting, and join lines with newline separators to improve LLM prompt fidelity and line integrity.

steps.operation: Refactor per-line LLM input assembly to use newline-joined per-line content and enforce one-to-one mapping between input lines and corrected_lines
steps.reason: Token-based segmentation replaces brittle character-based splitting; joining lines with a newline separator ensures stable per-line contexts for the LLM and maintains correct alignment between input lines and the model's corrected output lines, as described in the hotfix.
steps.side_effects: Tests and integration points that relied on the old per-line input format may require updates; downstream span construction logic may need to adapt to the new line-aligned outputs; ensure the LLM prompt construction remains compatible with token-based segmentation.

steps.operation: Remove character-count based line-splitting in text processing and align continuation/merging with token-based segmentation; preserve line boundaries via newline joining
steps.reason: Ensure that line handling uses token-based segmentation rather than character counts, removing brittle char-based splitting and aligning with the updated LLM input assembly in llm_text.py.
steps.side_effects: Tests relying on the old character-based splitting behavior may need updates; potential changes to how merged text blocks map to spans; verify that line boundaries are preserved and no unintended blank lines or trailing spaces are introduced.
